home.heroText:
I help apps, SaaS, and aggressive-growth startups turn paid social
into a real growth channel.
home.subtext: End-to-end performance creative — strategy, production, and the operational system behind it.
home.openTo: Open to fractional engagements with brands and agencies serious about paid social.
home.stat2Label: daily paying users scaled in 9 months

work.title: Work
work.sw_subtitle: Scaled daily paying users 4–5x in nine months
work.sw_summary: Built the creative operation from zero, scaled the flagship product, and ran every weekly performance read. From no marketing team to a full creative pipeline.
work.am_subtitle: BFCM creative direction and the mid-campaign pivot that worked
work.am_summary: Stepped in for Black Friday, wrote briefs for editors, designers, and UGC creators. Pivoted mid-campaign when the data showed evergreen outperforming BFCM-specific creative.
work.ab_subtitle: 41% host signup lift, 58% employer signup lift
work.ab_summary: Drove signup growth through front-end performance creative testing despite limited conversion attribution. Results measured on front-end signals.

simplywise.title: SimplyWise: scaled Cost Estimator's daily paying users 4–5x in nine months
simplywise.engagement: Creative strategist and producer for SimplyWise, a US tech company building AI-powered apps for self-employed pros and small business owners. I own concept development, creative testing, account-level performance analysis, and creator management across paid social — the primary growth channel.
simplywise.challenge1: No marketing team. No creative system. The flagship product was at a low daily paying user count with no reliable pipeline to scale from.
simplywise.challenge2: The audience changes by product, and each one needs creative anchored in real user language, not invented benefits. Every concept has to clear Andromeda and survive CBO routing that defaults spend to historical winners.
simplywise.challenge3: Building everything from scratch while running a live account at the same time.
simplywise.approach1: Built the creative operation from scratch — set up the Notion concept database, our naming convention, brief and QC templates, the asset library that the entire team now uses in ad production, and the handoff system between strategy, production, and media buying
simplywise.approach2: Ran weekly, quarterly, and annual performance analysis — pulled directly from Meta Ads Manager, TikTok Ads Manager, and Motion, translated into actionable feedback the team worked from
simplywise.approach3: Built a Flask app in Claude Code that drafts, scores, and QCs creative briefs before they go to production — cut the cycle from days to hours per concept
simplywise.approach4: Ran the Spanish-language expansion as a parallel pipeline, not a translated afterthought — separate research, separate creative, same performance accountability
simplywise.approach5: Built out the in-house UGC production — sourced authentic content from real app users, briefed and directed creators, and managed those relationships end-to-end
simplywise.result1: Daily paying users on the flagship product scaled 4–5x over nine months
simplywise.result2: Iterated on the historical top-performing creative — the new version surpassed the original and held as a top creative across the account for months
simplywise.result3: Top static I produced ran at roughly a third of the campaign average CPR
simplywise.result4: Secondary product's blended CPR improved by more than 50%
simplywise.result5: Weekly performance reports surfaced the CBO spend-concentration issue that was starving new creative — informed the campaign restructure conversation with the founder
simplywise.takeaway: I don't just write ads — I build the system that produces them, then read the data coming back out of it.

awesomeMaps.title: Awesome Maps: BFCM creative direction and the mid-campaign pivot that worked
awesomeMaps.engagement: Awesome Maps is a DTC e-commerce brand specializing in unique travel products featuring their hand-drawn world maps. I first started working with them as a UGC creator in Q1 2024. Through consistent overdelivery and strategic input on their ad performance, I built a strong relationship with the team. By Q4, they brought me in as their Creative Strategist for Black Friday / Cyber Monday.
awesomeMaps.challenge: A small DTC brand heading into BFCM needed fresh creative concepts and disciplined ad performance analysis — but the assumption that BFCM-specific creative would outperform evergreen turned out to be wrong mid-campaign.
awesomeMaps.approach1: Refined the ad creative strategy based on past performance data
awesomeMaps.approach2: Developed new ad concepts and iterated on top performers
awesomeMaps.approach3: Wrote detailed briefs for video editors, designers, and UGC creators and oversaw production end-to-end
awesomeMaps.approach4: Analyzed live performance and pivoted the strategy mid-flight — shifted spend from net-new BFCM concepts back into scaling evergreen content when the data showed evergreen was outperforming

abroadly.title: Abroadly: 41% host signup lift and 58% employer signup lift on limited attribution
abroadly.engagement: Abroadly is a tech startup in the travel space — a social networking platform connecting budget travelers with accommodations, jobs, companions, meetups, and travel guides to promote sustainable travel and cultural exchange. I led their paid social creative strategy, focused on driving signups for both hosts and employers.
abroadly.challenge: Limited conversion attribution made it hard to see exactly where users were dropping off in the funnel. Creative decisions had to be made on front-end signals alone — which means the creative itself had to do more work.
abroadly.approach1: Analyzed front-end engagement metrics as the primary signal given limited downstream attribution
abroadly.approach2: Tested creative variations across different audience targeting segments to identify what resonated with each
abroadly.approach3: Iterated on the formats and hooks that drove the strongest front-end engagement
abroadly.approach4: Worked closely with the media buyer to identify winning creatives and refine messaging for ad efficiency

services.title: Services
services.subtitle: Two ways I work with brands and agencies.
services.cs_description: End-to-end creative operations for paid social — research, concept development, production oversight, performance analysis, and iteration. I embed with your team and own the full creative loop.
services.ugc_description: UGC is actually how I started. I write, film, edit, and deliver finished UGC-style ad creative end-to-end, anchored in real audience research and built for performance.
services.notSureSubtext: Tell me where you are and what you need — we'll figure it out.

cs.title: Creative Strategy
cs.subtitle: End-to-end performance creative strategy for brands and agencies running paid social as a primary acquisition channel.
cs.include1: Creative strategy and research — reputation analysis, customer review mining, competitor analysis, past-performance data analysis
cs.include3: Creator and influencer sourcing, briefing, and management
cs.include5: End-to-end static and video creation using AI tools (like Higgsfield), plus video editing
cs.include6: Writing detailed creative briefs and managing video editors and graphic designers through to delivery
cs.include7: Ad performance monitoring, reporting, and creative iteration
cs.include8: Building the operational systems that make all of the above repeatable
cs.whoText: Good fit if you're a brand or agency running paid social as a primary acquisition channel, and you need someone who can own the creative side of it end-to-end — from strategy through to production.
cs.howText: Monthly retainer engagement. Starts with an intake form so I can learn about your business. If it's a fit, I'll follow up to set up a discovery call.
cs.faq1q: What kinds of companies do I work best with?
cs.faq1a: Brands and agencies running paid social as a primary acquisition channel, where ad creative is the lever that needs to move — and the team needs someone who can own it end-to-end.
cs.faq2q: What kinds of companies aren't a good fit?
cs.faq2a: Startups still figuring out who their customer is or what makes their product worth buying, brands not yet running consistent paid social, and teams looking for "set it and forget it" creative. The work I do requires a real testing cadence and a willingness to act on what the data shows.
cs.faq3q: What's actually included in a retainer?
cs.faq3a: Creative strategy and research, concept development, ad scripting, creator sourcing and briefing, UGC production management, end-to-end static and video creation using AI tools (like Higgsfield) plus video editing, performance analysis pulled directly from your ad managers, and ongoing creative iteration. I do not handle media buying or brand identity work.
cs.faq4a: Success metrics depend on the business. We define them together at kickoff — usually some mix of ad-side metrics (CPR, CTR, in-app start trials, mobile app installs, website start trials) and what those translate to downstream.
cs.faq5q: How does a typical engagement start?
cs.faq5a: Submit the intake form so I can learn about your business → if it's a fit I'll shoot you an email to set up a discovery call → 30-day kickoff with creative audit and concept roadmap → ongoing monthly rhythm of analysis → concept → produce → test.
cs.ctaSubtext: Fill out the intake form — I'll be in touch.

ugc.title: UGC
ugc.intro1: UGC is actually how I started. I was the face of the ads — and after a while, I realized I didn't want to just deliver someone else's brief. I wanted to be the one writing it.
ugc.intro2: So I moved into strategy. Now I bring my creator-side instincts to every brief I write, regardless of whether I'm the on-camera talent or not — scripts that don't sound like scripts, hooks rooted in consumer psychology, formats that actually convert.
ugc.what1: I write, film, edit, and deliver finished UGC-style ad creative end-to-end. Scripts and concepts can come from me or from your brief — I work both ways. If you only need raw footage and want to handle editing in-house, that's an option too.
ugc.what2: Every piece is built around a hook → demonstration → conversion arc, anchored in real audience research, and produced in formats your media buyer can actually test. Available in English and Polish.
ugc.sponsored_heading: I also offer sponsored content for brands in travel, lifestyle, and anything Asia-related.
ugc.sponsored_p1: My audience on @nomadicmarta is built around discovering places and experiences across Asia.
ugc.sponsored_p2: You get organic reach, real audience trust, and a different kind of credibility than paid ads can buy.
ugc.sponsored_mobile_p2: Organic reach, real audience trust, and a different kind of credibility than paid ads can buy.
ugc.faq1a: Anywhere that fits the brief. Home, gym, coffee shops, retail stores, authentic Thai markets, jungle, waterfalls, outdoor pools, urban streets, hotels, beaches. I'm based in Chiang Mai, Thailand and can travel within Thailand or further with prior planning. Location complexity affects rate and turnaround.
ugc.faq2q: Do you write the script, or should I provide one?
ugc.faq2a: I write scripts and briefs as part of my standard scope. If you have a script you want me to deliver as-is, I offer script reads.
ugc.faq3a: Each project scopes differently depending on what I'm doing — scripting, editing, hook variations, location shoots, asset count. For a typical engagement: about a week to write the brief, client review, then another week to deliver finals. Rush fees available if you need it faster.
ugc.faq4q: What's your usage rights policy for paid ads?
ugc.faq4a: Usage rights are built into my fee and scoped to the length of usage you need. We'll align on the period before kickoff.
ugc.ctaSubtext: Tell me about your brand and what you're running.

about.title: About
about.bio1: I'm a freelance performance creative strategist working with brands and agencies on the creative side of paid social.
about.bio2: I started as a UGC creator — I was the face of the ads. After a while, I realized I didn't want to just deliver someone else's brief. I wanted to be the one writing it. So I moved into strategy, and now I bring my creator-side instincts to every brief I write — hooks that actually stop the scroll, scripts that perform on camera, real understanding of what makes UGC convert.
about.bio3: My background is hybrid — agency experience plus in-house consulting — across Meta and TikTok. I currently work as the performance creative strategist for SimplyWise, a US tech company building AI apps for self-employed professionals.
about.bio4: I'm based in Thailand and work remotely with teams globally. I work in English and Polish (native), and write Spanish-language briefs with AI assistance — useful for running ad pipelines that reach Spanish-speaking audiences.
about.ctaSubtext: Fill out the intake form — I'll be in touch.
